title: Hong Kong conglomerates and post-pandemic balance-sheet discipline
author: Portfolio research note
summary: Short note on how Jardine Swire and Cathay frame disposals recovery and capital allocation in recent reporting
subject: Business history and corporate finance
keyIdea: Strong recent performance matters less than whether disposal-led gains turn into durable operating returns and clearer capital-allocation discipline

title: Euro-area stability and the lived economics of younger households
author: Portfolio research note
summary: Synthesis note linking inflation youth unemployment and housing pressure to the macroeconomic credibility younger Europeans are likely to care about
subject: European macroeconomics
keyIdea: Macroeconomic stability is judged politically through affordability labour-market entry and public services rather than headline inflation targets alone

title: Gilts ETFs and the problem of comparing labels instead of functions
author: Portfolio research note
summary: Reading note on why direct gilts gilt ETFs and broad market ETFs serve different investor purposes even when they appear in the same comparison
subject: Investment analysis
keyIdea: The relevant comparison starts with purpose and cash-flow structure because an ETF is a wrapper while a gilt is an asset with defined contractual terms
